title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.

email_summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months. Severity is rated critical.
card_summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.

title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
summary: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmore warned that similar capabilities are weeks or months from proliferation. CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report puts average eCrime breakout time at 29 minutes. Mandiant's M-Trends

title: Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
summary: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive state-sponsored meddling in infrastructure that is finally coming to light. title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
summary: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.
email_summary: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.
card_summary: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.

title: CISA Adds Seven Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.
email_summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.
card_summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.

title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
summary: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.

sections.context: This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.

email_summary: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.
card_summary: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.

title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
summary: The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added them as friends on the social media platform, turning the trust-building exercise into a delivery channel for a remote access trojan called RokRAT.

sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

Severity is rated high.

title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
summary: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.
email_summary: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.

title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
summary: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.
email_summary: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.

title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-the-shelf toolkit called W3LL to steal thousands of victims' account credentials and attempt more than $20 million in fraud.

title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
summary: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.
email_summary: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.

title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
summary: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT.
email_summary: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT.
